title: Deterministic Tests, Fault Injection, Contract Evidence, and Observability · Go Client Systems · Go Semantics Case: Fault Injection
summary: A client library release needs RoundTripper fakes, httptest servers, TLS fixtures, truncation faults, contract tests, and low-cardinality telemetry. Planned artifact: Add and verify the http go fault injection increment in a Go 1.26 net/http fault-injected client verification harness. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Inject cancellation, timeout, reset, partial body, malformed fields, bad media type, redirect, and retry exhaustion deterministically. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.

Verify documented provider and consumer contracts against changed schemas, status codes, and version boundaries. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Go Predict Fault Injection · Theory Fault Injection
steps.instruction:
theory case — Go fleet evidence model test G-4 injects a delayed header, truncated gzip body, malformed JSON, cross-origin redirect, and cancellation. Assert the outgoing request, cleanup, failure taxonomy, attempt count, low-cardinality telemetry, and no secret leakage.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Fault Injection contract for the supplied data.
